title: Immutable Work Item migration boundary

sources.reason: Use row-owned immutable evidence instead of a mutable migration sequence cutoff.
Calibrate the measured repository line ceiling after the implementation.
Record the two low-risk findings from the new subprocess boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: Changing legacy archive classification must preserve historical readability and strict checks.

executionDecision.reason: The migration boundary is explicit in immutable row evidence and baseCommit semantics.

preReviewWarnings: Review legacy archive rows and ensure no historical files were rewritten.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Archive integrity classification no longer depends on a mutable global sequence threshold.
Historical rows remain readable and rows with immutable digests receive strict validation.
Governance complexity tests and full quality checks pass.
The migration boundary is documented for future Work Items.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: A mutable archive sequence cutoff can silently change which historical evidence is trusted.
constraints: Do not rewrite historical archive files.
intent.rationale: Let each immutable archive row declare strict evidence through its own digest pair.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized fully automatic remediation and approved governance-policy edits within WI-07 scope.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
